CREATE TABLE ip_ranges(id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, ip inet);
SELECT sys.left_shift_assign(ip, inet '192.168.1/24') FROM ip_ranges WHERE ip = inet '192.168.1.0/24';
CREATE TABLE ip_checks(id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, ip1 inet, ip2 inet);
SELECT CASE WHEN sys.left_shift_assign(ip1, ip2) THEN 'Contained' ELSE 'Not Contained' END FROM ip_checks WHERE ip1 = inet '192.168.1.0/24';
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ip_ranges WHERE sys.left_shift_assign(ip, inet '192.168.1/24');
CREATE TABLE subnet_overlap(ip_range1 inet, ip_range2 inet);
SELECT sys.left_shift_assign(ip_range1, ip_range2) FROM subnet_overlap WHERE ip_range1 = inet '192.168.1.0/24' AND ip_range2 = inet '192.168.1.0/24';
WITH valid_ips AS (SELECT ip FROM ip_ranges WHERE sys.left_shift_assign(ip, inet '192.168.1/24')) SELECT ip FROM valid_ips;
SELECT DISTINCT ip FROM ip_ranges WHERE sys.left_shift_assign(ip, inet '192.168.1/24') AND ip != inet '192.168.1.1/24';
